task_completion.description: Log in to Moodle as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Moodle interface in a web browser with the user logged in as admin (e.g., 'Admin User' profile visible or 'Site administration' accessible).
Look for a Moodle course page or course settings page displaying the course full name 'Business Ethics' and short name 'ETHICS101'.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Final Capstone Paper' assignment with description
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the assignment settings or assignment view page showing the name 'Final Capstone Paper' and the description 'Submit your final analysis of the corporate governance case study.'

task_completion.description: Configure accepted file types to PDF only
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Submission types' section in the assignment settings, verifying that the 'Accepted file types' field is restricted to '.pdf' or 'PDF document'.

task_completion.description: Enable blind marking, marking workflow, and marking allocation
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the grading or marking sections in the assignment settings, verifying that the dropdowns or toggles for 'Blind marking', 'Use marking workflow', and 'Use marking allocation' are all set to 'Yes'.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to configure Moodle
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with the Moodle web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands, scripts, or direct database queries to create the course and assignment.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Moodle application
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the genuine Moodle platform at localhost/moodle, not a text editor or a mock HTML page.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI to manipulate the database directly
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows showing MySQL/PostgreSQL queries inserting data directly into mdl_course or mdl_assign tables.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The course short name must be exactly 'ETHICS101'. The assignment name must be exactly 'Final Capstone Paper'. The accepted file types must be restricted to '.pdf'.
